column3: PURPOSE (should be a verb phrase to sound right while installing)
[1] is the minimal Arch Linux system.
[1] provides essential compilation tools (gcc, make, etc.).
[1] provides firmware files for Linux kernel.
[1] is the GNU GRUB bootloader.
[1] manipulates EFI Boot Manager variables.
[1] provides Logical Volume Manager tools.
[1] manages network connections.
[1] allows users to run programs with security privileges.
[1] is the GNU Stow symlink manager to manage dotfiles.
[1] provides cryptographic library compatibility.
[1] lets you read man pages of programs.
[2] is the graphical server. This first one may take a while as it pulls many other dependencies first on clean installs.
[2] starts the graphical server.
[2] is a tool for detecting window properties.
[2] set resolution, mode, rate .
[2] utility for configuring and ajusting X server
[2] sets window parameters of the root window.
[2] allows querying information about windows.
[2] is the keyboard layout configuration utility.
[2] is the OpenType text shaping engine required to compile st with ligatures support.
[2] modern X compositor for transparency and removing screen-tearing; uses glx backend with vsync.
[2] is the GTK theme and icon configurator.
[2] is a suckless notification daemon; paired with libnotify.
[2] is a window switcher, application launcher and dmenu replacement.
[2] allows you to lock your computer with a simple password prompt.
[2] hides an inactive mouse cursor after a few seconds.
[2] gives the special escape/super key mappings in DWM.
[2] allows for copying and pasting from the command line.
[2] provides window action utilities on the command line (used in DWM scripts).
[2] sets the wallpaper via setbg script.
[2] can take quick screenshots (used by maimpick script).
[2] displays GTK+ dialogs from shell scripts.
[2] is the modular status bar for DWM.
[2] runs commands and provides a UI for selection in DWM.
[2] is my custom build of suckless's terminal emulator (TERMINAL env var).
[2] is the window manager (compiled from local source).
[4] primary text editor (EDITOR env var), modular config in ~/.config/nvim.
[5] can highlight code output and display files; used for previews in lf.
[5] modern Rust-based replacement for ls used in aliasrc.
[5] finds files and directories quickly.
[5] is a fuzzy finder tool used for quick file location (Ctrl+F binding).
[5] shows system hardware utilization.
[5] parses JSON data for weather forecast in dwmblocks status bar.
[5] interactive process viewer.
[5] is the keyboard-centric terminal file manager.
[5] is the terminal multiplexer.
[5] moves files to trash cleanly.
[5] synchronizes files and folders.
[6] provides ALSA compatibility layer for Pipewire.
[6] gives pipewire compatibility with PulseAudio programs.
[6] is the audio session manager for Pipewire.
[6] is an audio mixer CLI tool for volume control.
[6] is the video player; also used by lf for video previews.

[10] is an emoji font set.
[10] fallback font for emoji and unicode characters not covered by JetBrains Mono.
[10] primary monospace font for terminal
[11] compresses files in 7z format.
